steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The subject is the noun phrase the country's name, which is singular. The blank must be filled with a verb that connects this subject to the adjective pronounced.

steps.title: Apply the rule of subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. The subject is "the country's name" — singular. The sentence states a permanent fact, so simple present is appropriate. Rule: a singular subject takes a singular verb.
  • Choice A — "is": Singular simple present. Correctly agrees with the singular subject "name." ✓
  • Choice B — "are being": Plural progressive — wrong in both number (plural) and aspect (progressive implies a temporary ongoing action, not a fixed pronunciation rule). ✗
  • Choice C — "have been": Plural perfect — wrong number, and perfect tense implies a recently completed action rather than a standing fact. ✗
  • Choice D — "are": Plural simple present — wrong number for the singular subject. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
